No Poverty.reason: The course discusses the '1997 East Asian economic crisis' (Week 11), which directly relates to Target 1.5 by increasing vulnerability to economic shocks for the poor.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture was found in the course syllabus.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to health, well-being, disease prevention, or healthcare was found in the course syllabus.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education. Its focus on understanding Korean society and culture promotes knowledge for global citizenship and cultural diversity (Target 4.7).
evidence: 한국 사회와 문화를 이해하는데 핵심적인 이슈들

Gender Equality.reason: Week 8 explicitly focuses on 'Family and Women' (가족과여성), examining their roles and status in society, directly addressing gender equality (Targets 5.1, 5.c).

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to water, sanitation, hygiene, or water quality was found in the course syllabus.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency was found in the course syllabus.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course directly analyzes 'Korean economic growth' (Week 6) and the '1997 East Asian economic crisis' (Week 11), addressing national economic productivity and policies (Targets 8.1, 8.2).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course examines Korea's 'modernization process' and 'internet expansion' (Week 13), relating to industrialization (Target 9.2) and access to information technology (Target 9.c).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course explicitly covers 'Korean multiculturalism' (Week 14), 'everyday racism' and 'hate sentiments' (Week 15), directly addressing social inclusion and eliminating discrimination (Targets 10.2, 10.3).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to urban development, housing, transport, or cultural heritage was found in the course syllabus.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to sustainable consumption, waste management, or resource efficiency was found in the course syllabus.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to climate change, mitigation, adaptation, or disaster resilience was found in the course syllabus.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable fisheries was found in the course syllabus.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, forests, or land degradation was found in the course syllabus.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course fundamentally examines 'colonial experience', 'political democratization' (Week 7), and 'national division' (Week 5), which are central to peace, justice, and institutional development (Targets 16.3, 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course discusses 'globalization' and the '1997 East Asian economic crisis' (Week 11), which relate to global macroeconomic stability and international policy coherence (Target 17.13).
